# Security Policy

## Reporting a Vulnerability

If you discover a security vulnerability in this repository, please **do not** open a public GitHub issue or describe the exploit in a PR comment — this is a public repository and doing so would expose the vulnerability to anyone watching.

Instead, please open a private security advisory:

**[Report a vulnerability →](https://github.com/JamesMillerBlog/blog/security/advisories/new)**

GitHub private advisories allow us to discuss, patch, and coordinate disclosure without exposing details publicly.

## What to include

- Description of the vulnerability (type, location)
- Steps to reproduce or proof of concept
- Potential impact
- Suggested fix if you have one

## Automated security reviews

All pull requests can be reviewed by an automated AI security auditor by commenting `@claude review` on the PR. The reviewer is configured to flag critical findings privately rather than publishing exploit details in public comments.
